lines.text: I'm positioned north of the Beltline on Todd Drive. {{ELAPSED}} in, and we're tracking seven active fire zones across the isthmus. Visibility is down to maybe a quarter-mile due to smoke drift.
Madison-wide cumulative kill count is holding at 139. That's seventeen kills this hour — slightly down from last year's 22-kill bracket at this point, but the fire-start rate is up twelve percent.

East Side still carrying weight. Atwood corridor is at 34 confirmed, which matches 2168 exactly through hour eight. Willy Street holding steady at the lower end — the neighborhood watch perimeter is still intact.
Tucker, can you speak to the fire methodology? Are these structural starts or incendiary?

Mix. State Street ruins have three confirmed structure fires — looks like coordinated action, same pattern we saw in 2168 at the 23-hour mark but compressed. Two vehicle fires on the Beltline proper. Two outliers — one appears to be an apartment complex on Park Street, and one is... hold on, my body-cam is cutting—

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide: 139 cumulative. East Side 34. Hour 8 delta: +17 YoY vs +22 (2168).
Seven active fire zones. Smoke visibility reduced to 0.25 miles. Air quality alert issued for lake districts.
State Street: 3 structure fires, coordinated start pattern. Willy Street: barricade holding. Park Street: 1 apartment complex fire, unconfirmed cause.
Weather note: NW wind 18 mph pushing smoke toward downtown. Cold front holding. Fog off Mendota now visible from the Beltline.
